channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rubio says US is revoking all visas held by South Sudanese passport holders
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the United States is revoking all visas held by South Sudanese passport holders, accusing the African nation’s government of “taking advantage of the United States.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu will meet again Monday on Gaza, tariffs and more
item.description: PALM BEACH GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump plans to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday in what would be their second White House sit-down since Trump's return to office.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Layoffs threaten US firefighter cancer registry, mine research and mask lab
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Government staffing cuts have gutted a small U.S. health agency that aims to protect workers — drawing rebukes from firefighters, coal miners, medical equipment manufacturers and a range of others.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NY public schools tell Trump administration they won't comply with DEI order
item.description: New York state school officials have told the Trump administration that they will not comply with its demands to end diversity, equity and inclusion practices, despite the administration’s threats to terminate federal education funding.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Congress pushes back at White House over Signal group chat
item.description: The White House’s assertion that the case is closed surrounding national security officials’ use of Signal is getting pushback in Congress, even among some Republicans who say their own probes will press on.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Angry protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators from New York to Alaska protested against President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate kicks off all-night 'vote-a-rama' as Democrats plan to press GOP on Trump, DOGE
item.description: Senate Republicans triggered a vote-a-rama on Friday in a bid to pass Trump's budget resolution after it ends.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom pursues trade partnerships to shield California from tariff impact
item.description: Governor Gavin Newsom's office announced on Friday plans to shield California's economy from the ripple effects of the Trump administration's escalating tariff war.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington and Oregon sue over Trump elections order, saying mail voters could be disenfranchised
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Washington and Oregon on Friday became the latest states to ask a court to reject President Donald Trump’s efforts to overhaul elections in the U.S., a day after Democratic officials in 19 others filed a similar lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge targeted by GOP for impeachment deals blow to Trump's FEMA objectives
item.description: A Rhode Island federal judge ordered the Trump administration to unfreeze federal funds after plaintiffs argued it defied a prior court order to release the money.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Interior secretary orders national parks to be open and accessible as workforce is cut
item.description: Under criticism for staff cuts across the country, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum is directing national parks to “remain open and accessible” and says officials will ensure proper staffing to do so.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans are debating a higher tax rate for millionaires: Report
item.description: Republicans are reportedly considering raising taxes on millionaires to help offset the cost of their tax bill.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ asks for pause of order for man in El Salvador to be returned to US
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia is among the Salvadoran migrants who have been deported due to suspected gang affiliations.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Stock market tumble impacts retirement savings; Florida economists explain
item.description: “This panic, or phase, is not going to last more than 30 days. The stock market, sooner than later, will bounce back,” said Tampa Economist Federico Alves.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and ending any discriminatory diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: National Security Council staffers fired in wake of Trump meeting with Laura Loomer
item.description: Multiple staff members on the White House National Security Council were fired Thursday following a meeting President Trump had with far-right activist Laura Loomer where she raised questions about their loyalty.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Guns, mental health concerns a difficult mix in proposed Nevada law
item.description: Gun rights advocates spoke Wednesday against proposed legislation meant to keep weapons away from people experiencing a mental health crisis. It's a murky area of Nevada law that even makes police uncomfortable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: E-Verify legislation starts to move at the statehouse, passing its first committee
item.description: The state legislature is now at the halfway point in the 2025 legislative session and as the end swiftly approaches, lawmakers in Tallahassee are pushing for more immigration reform.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Auditor reports that Minnesota muni liquor store profits up, in part due to THC offerings
item.description: Minnesota State Auditor Julie Blaha issued her annual report on municipal-run liquor store sales. It was the first include sales of hemp-derived THC beverages and gummies, and operators suggested those items contributed to store profits in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge drops corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: Adams was scheduled to go on trial in April until new leadership at the Justice Department under the Trump administration ordered prosecutors in New York in February to drop the case, sparking a public outcry and resignations of prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas and Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams federal bribery case dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams' federal bribery case has been dismissed, court records show. In a letter filed Wednesday, Judge Dale Ho ordered the case dropped with prejudice, meaning the government cannot bring these charges against Adams again in the future.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Ohio school district reverts to birth names, catching students, teachers off guard
item.description: Students at Columbus City Schools with preferred names in the district's system had their names reverted back to match their birth certificates.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, Fox 5

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: Luna accuses House Freedom Caucus of smear; exits group over parental proxy voting battle
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) announced Monday that she is leaving the House Freedom Caucus amid her battle with GOP leadership and hard-line Republicans over her push to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC
